description_long: This is a weathered, dark stone torso of a human figure, missing its head and lower legs, with a draped garment carved in low relief. The back of the object features a vertical panel of deeply incised hieroglyphic inscriptions, suggesting an ancient Egyptian origin. The stone surface is rough and uneven, with visible chips and erosion, especially around the shoulders and arms. The left arm is bent and partially preserved, while the right arm is mostly missing. The base is flat and appears to have been cut or mounted for display, contrasting with the more organic, worn upper portion. The overall form is monolithic and solid, with no visible cavities or openings.
description: A weathered stone torso of a draped human figure with hieroglyphic inscriptions on the back, missing its head and lower legs. The surface is rough and eroded, with a flat base for mounting.
description_view_0: The front view shows the draped garment, missing head, and partial left arm, highlighting the worn and eroded details of the torso.
description_view_1: The left side view emphasizes the thickness of the stone and the drapery folds, with the arm and garment contours visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a vertical panel of hieroglyphic inscriptions running down the back, with the flat base clearly visible.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the curvature of the back and the missing right arm, with the garment's edge and surface texture apparent.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a plinth or pedestal
attribute_pattern: Deeply incised hieroglyphic carvings on the back, with irregular drapery folds on the front; the patterns are artificial and visually prominent.
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded with visible chips and surface wear, indicating significant exposure to natural elements over time.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts sharply with modern urban features but may be partially surrounded by landscaping or informational signage in a curated outdoor setting.
